topic: Bangkok heat rant — food spoilage worries

turns.content: I am DYING na ka 🥵🥵 Bangkok is trying to kill me today. 39 degrees. I am standing next to a stove cooking chicken and I feel like I am the one being roasted haha

39 degrees AND standing next to a stove?! That's brutal. April in Bangkok is always the worst but 39 is extreme even by Thai standards. Are you drinking enough water? How's the rest of the day looking?

I drink water all day na ka don't worry. but the real problem is not me — it's the FOOD. this heat make everything spoil so fast. I am so stressed about it. this morning I prep chicken at 5am and by 10am the ones I haven't cooked yet already feel warm. my ice is melting in like 2 hours instead of 4. everything is a fight against the heat right now 😫

That's a real concern — food safety in this kind of heat is no joke. Chicken especially is risky because bacteria multiply fast above 40C, and your prep area on the street doesn't have AC. A few things that might help:

1. **Prep in smaller batches** — instead of prepping everything at 5am, do it in 2-3 rounds throughout the morning
2. **More ice, better insulation** — can you get a better cooler box or wrap your current one in foil blankets?
3. **Frozen bottles** — freeze water bottles and pack them around the raw chicken instead of loose ice. They last longer and don't make everything wet.

What's your current setup for keeping things cold at the cart?

I have a styrofoam box with ice for the raw chicken and another one for the cucumber and sauce ingredient. but the styrofoam is thin na ka. the good cooler boxes are expensive and also heavy to carry. I already carry so much stuff to the cart every morning. but you right, the frozen bottle idea is good!! I can freeze like 10 big bottles at home

The frozen bottle trick is a game-changer — they keep things cold way longer than loose ice, they don't create a watery mess as they melt, and you can refreeze them every night. Just fill 10-15 big bottles and rotate them.

For the styrofoam box, here's a cheap upgrade: wrap the outside in aluminum foil or that silver bubble insulation you can get at hardware stores. It reflects heat and can add 1-2 extra hours of cold. Costs almost nothing and doesn't add weight.

ooh the foil wrap is smart na ka!! I will try that. but honestly the real nightmare is the sauce ingredient. my fresh chili and garlic and ginger, if they sit in the heat too long they get soft and the flavour change. and the cucumber go limp haha. nobody want limp cucumber on their plate 😂

Haha, limp cucumber is definitely not Instagram-worthy! For the fresh ingredients, a damp cloth over them can actually help through evaporative cooling — same principle as sweating. Wrap the cucumbers and fresh herbs in a damp towel inside the cooler box and they'll stay crisp longer.

For the chili and garlic, you could pre-prep and keep them in small sealed containers in the cooler. Less air exposure means they hold up better. Do you usually prep all your garnishes at once or as you go?

I prep most thing in the morning na ka because during lunch rush I have no time to cut anything. it's just cook serve cook serve nonstop. so the cucumber is cut at 6am and some of it doesn't get used until 1pm. that is 7 hours sitting in Bangkok heat!! even with ice box it's tough

7 hours in this heat is a long stretch for cut cucumber. Here's a practical approach: split your cucumber prep into two batches. Cut half at 6am for the morning service. Then during any lull between 10-11am (before the lunch rush hits), quickly cut the second batch. That way the afternoon cucumbers are only 2-3 hours old instead of 7.

It's 5 minutes of extra work but the quality difference is huge — crisp cucumbers versus sad ones. Your customers will notice, and your photos will look better too.

you are right na ka. I am going to start doing 2 batch cucumber from now on. honestly the hot season make me appreciate the cool season SO much. December and January I don't think about any of this. but April... April is war haha. okay I feel better now that I have a plan. frozen bottles, foil wrap, 2 batch cucumber. survival mode na ka!! 💪🥵

April is absolutely war for outdoor food vendors — you're not being dramatic! But you've got a solid heat-survival kit now: frozen bottles, foil insulation, split-batch prep. All cheap and easy to do.

Hang in there — May should bring the rain and some relief. Until then, keep hydrating yourself too, not just your cucumbers! And maybe grab one of those small portable fans for the cart if you can — even a clip-on battery one helps. Stay safe out there! 💪

the portable fan!! yes I saw one on Shopee for like 350 baht, clip-on type na ka. I was not sure if it worth it but you know what, my comfort matter too haha. okay going to order it tonight. I survive this April and come out stronger 😤💪 thank you na ka!!

350 baht for a clip-on fan is totally worth it — you'll wonder why you didn't get one sooner. Your comfort IS important, you can't serve great food if you're passing out from heat! Order it tonight, and you'll be the coolest vendor on the soi — literally. Stay strong, Pan! 💪

haha 'coolest vendor on the soi' 😂 I like that. okay ordering now. next April I will be fully prepared — frozen bottles, foil box, 2 batch cucumber, AND a fan. bring it on Bangkok!! 🥵💪 goodnight na ka!!
That's the full survival loadout right there! Next April you'll be coaching OTHER vendors on how to beat the heat. Goodnight, Pan — stay cool! 💪
